motivation: The user needed to install/build/run the project and connect it to a custom API proxy using a provided key/base_url, then understand how to continue improving the fork with clear evidence-based criteria and whether to rely on previously decompiled sources.
approach: Chose to configure the CLI via the built-in user settings mechanism (`~/.claude/settings.json`), leveraging `settings.env` to inject `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, and validated behavior with real CLI runs and raw `curl` requests. For future development direction, proposed a layered “source of truth” hierarchy (runtime bundle/sourcemap → repo mainline src → restored-src reference) and concrete criteria for judging progress (reachability, E2E smoke, stub traversal on hot paths, bundle fidelity, regressions).
challenges: The main blocker was a mis-specified base URL including `/v1`, causing requests to double-prefix (`/v1/v1/messages`); fixing `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to the root resolved it, while an alternate endpoint (`lingkong.xyz`) returned 502 and was deemed unusable.
tradeoffs: Prioritized operational correctness and fidelity to the packaged runtime over fully backfilling all stubs or mirroring restored-src wholesale, accepting that some stub-heavy areas and non-core modules may remain incomplete until they block real, reachable user flows.
outcome: Confirmed the repo supports user-level configuration at `~/.claude/settings.json` and successfully ran the CLI against the working proxy (`code.pumpkinai.vip`) after correcting the base URL format. Produced an evidence-backed plan for ongoing work: treat the packaged bundle/sourcemap as the ultimate behavioral reference, use restored-src only for targeted diffs on core hot paths, and evaluate “better” by runnable/verified behavior rather than by reducing stub counts.
